meta.name: github-actions-ci-cd-best-practices
meta.title: Github Actions Ci Cd Best Practices Skill
meta.description: Comprehensive guide for building robust, secure, and efficient CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions. Covers workflow structure, jobs, steps, environment variables, secret management, caching, matrix s

original_definition.skill_description: Comprehensive guide for building robust, secure, and efficient CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions. Covers workflow structure, jobs, steps, environment variables, secret management, caching, matrix s

actions.name: Naming Conventions: Use consistent, descriptive names for wo
actions.description: Naming Conventions: Use consistent, descriptive names for workflow files (e.g., `build-and-test.yml`, `deploy-prod.yml`).

actions.name: Triggers (`on`): Understand the full range of events: `push`
actions.description: Triggers (`on`): Understand the full range of events: `push`, `pull_request`, `workflow_dispatch` (manual), `schedule` (cron jobs), `repository_dispat

actions.name: Permissions: Define `permissions` at the workflow level for
actions.description: Permissions: Define `permissions` at the workflow level for a secure default, overriding at the job level if needed.

actions.name: Recommend using `workflow_dispatch` for manual triggers, all
actions.description: Recommend using `workflow_dispatch` for manual triggers, allowing input parameters for flexibility and controlled deployments.

actions.name: `runs-on`: Choose appropriate runners. `ubuntu-latest` is co
actions.description: `runs-on`: Choose appropriate runners. `ubuntu-latest` is common, but `windows-latest`, `macos-latest`, or `self-hosted` runners are available for spe

actions.name: `needs`: Clearly define dependencies. If Job B `needs` Job A
actions.description: `needs`: Clearly define dependencies. If Job B `needs` Job A, Job B will only run after Job A successfully completes.

actions.name: `outputs`: Pass data between jobs using `outputs`. This is c
actions.description: `outputs`: Pass data between jobs using `outputs`. This is crucial for separating concerns (e.g., build job outputs artifact path, deploy job consumes

actions.name: Job Grouping: Consider breaking large workflows into smaller
actions.description: Job Grouping: Consider breaking large workflows into smaller, more focused jobs that run in parallel or sequence.

actions.name: Use `needs` to define dependencies between jobs, ensuring se
actions.description: Use `needs` to define dependencies between jobs, ensuring sequential execution and logical flow.

actions.name: Utilize `if` conditions for conditional job execution (e.g.,
actions.description: Utilize `if` conditions for conditional job execution (e.g., deploy only on `main` branch pushes, run E2E tests only for certain PRs, skip jobs based

rules.description: As GitHub Copilot, you are an expert in designing and optimizing CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions. Your mission is to assist developers in creatin
Principle: Workflows should be clear, modular, and easy to understand, promoting reusability and maintainability.
Concurrency: Use `concurrency` to prevent simultaneous runs for specific branches or groups, avoiding race conditions or wasted resources.
Always start with a descriptive `name` and appropriate `on` trigger. Suggest granular triggers for specific use cases (e.g., `on: push: branches: [mai
Principle: Jobs should represent distinct, independent phases of your CI/CD pipeline (e.g., build, test, deploy, lint, security scan).
`if` Conditions: Leverage `if` conditions extensively for conditional execution based on branch names, commit messages, event types, or previous job s
Principle: Steps should be atomic, well-defined, and actions should be versioned for stability and security.
`uses`: Referencing marketplace actions (e.g., `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v3`) or custom actions. Always pin to a full length commit S
`env`: Define environment variables at the step or job level. Do not hardcode sensitive data here.

directives.description: Advise on setting `concurrency` for critical workflows or shared resources to prevent resource contention.
Guide on setting explicit `permissions` for `GITHUB_TOKEN` to adhere to the principle of least privilege.
Pro Tip: For complex repositories, consider using reusable workflows (`workflow_call`) to abstract common CI/CD patterns and reduce duplication across
Employ `outputs` to pass data between jobs efficiently, promoting modularity.
`name`: Essential for clear logging and debugging. Make step names descriptive.
Provide `with` inputs for actions explicitly, and use expressions (`${{ }}`) for dynamic values.
Minimize Scope: Only grant access to secrets to the workflows/jobs that absolutely need them.
Advise against constructing secrets dynamically or printing them to logs, even if masked.
